# Code of Conduct

## The short version

**Be decent.** Assume good faith. Give useful, specific feedback. Don't be a jerk.

## The slightly longer version

These are small, community-facing projects. Everyone here — the maintainer, contributors, users filing bugs — is doing this in their spare time. Interactions should reflect that.

### Expected

- Communicate with respect, even in disagreement
- Give concrete, actionable feedback ("the tool crashes when X" beats "this is broken")
- Assume the other person has good intent unless there's clear evidence otherwise
- Credit others' work when you build on it

### Not expected

- Personal attacks, harassment, or discrimination of any kind (race, gender, sexuality, ability, nationality, religion, age, appearance — any of it)
- Hostile or dismissive language in issues, PRs, or discussions
- Doxxing or sharing anyone's personal info
- Off-topic political flamewars

### Enforcement

If someone violates this in an issue, PR, or discussion:

1. First response: a warning, and usually the offending comment gets edited or removed
2. Repeat or severe violation: temporary or permanent ban from the repo, escalation to GitHub if warranted

The maintainer ([Godimas101](https://github.com/Godimas101)) has final say. Report violations by opening a private security advisory on the affected repo, or via [Patreon DM](https://patreon.com/Godimas101).

Adapted loosely from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/), kept small and specific to this community.
